title: Morning at the Harbour
summary: Mei runs a fish stall at an early morning harbour market, continuing a family trade passed down through three generations.
body: Before most people are awake, Mei drives her small van to the harbour. She parks near the water and unloads her boxes. Mei sells fish at the morning market. Her family has run this stall for three generations. She learned the trade from her mother, who learned it from her grandmother. Mei knows all the fishermen by name. They arrive just before dawn in their boats, and she buys the best fish directly from them. By six o'clock, her stall is full. She arranges the fish on beds of crushed ice and writes the prices on a small chalkboard. Customers start to arrive soon after. Some are chefs from local restaurants who need fresh fish for the lunchtime menu. Others are families buying food for the week. Mei knows most of her regular customers by name too. She wraps each purchase in paper and ties it with string. By nine o'clock, most of the fish is gone. She packs up, washes the stall with cold water, and drives home. She is tired, but she is happy. Her day's work is done before many people have had their breakfast.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for life experience
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Her family has run this stall for three generations' dùng present perfect vì hành động bắt đầu trong quá khứ và vẫn tiếp tục đến hôm nay.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Her family has run this stall for three generations' uses the present perfect because the action started in the past and continues to today.

translationVi: Trước khi hầu hết mọi người thức dậy, Mei lái chiếc xe tải nhỏ của mình đến bến cảng. Cô đỗ xe gần mặt nước và dỡ hàng. Mei bán cá tại chợ sáng. Gia đình cô đã duy trì quầy hàng này qua ba thế hệ. Cô học nghề từ mẹ, người đã học từ bà ngoại. Mei biết tên tất cả những ngư dân. Họ đến trước bình minh trên những chiếc thuyền của họ, và cô mua những con cá tươi nhất trực tiếp từ họ. Đến sáu giờ, quầy hàng của cô đã đầy. Cô xếp cá trên những lớp đá xay và viết giá lên một bảng đen nhỏ. Khách hàng bắt đầu đến ngay sau đó. Một số là đầu bếp từ các nhà hàng địa phương cần cá tươi cho thực đơn bữa trưa. Những người khác là các gia đình mua thực phẩm cho cả tuần. Mei cũng biết hầu hết khách hàng thường xuyên của mình theo tên. Cô gói mỗi hàng mua trong giấy và buộc bằng dây. Đến chín giờ, hầu hết cá đã bán hết. Cô thu dọn, rửa quầy hàng bằng nước lạnh và lái xe về nhà. Cô mệt, nhưng cô vui. Công việc trong ngày của cô đã xong trước khi nhiều người kịp ăn sáng.

questions.prompt: Where does Mei drive early in the morning?
options: To the market by the river
questions.explanation: 'Mei drives her small van to the harbour.'

questions.prompt: How long has Mei's family run the stall?
questions.explanation: 'Her family has run this stall for three generations.'

questions.prompt: Who did Mei learn the trade from?
questions.explanation: 'She learned the trade from her mother.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence about learning the trade.

questions.explanation: 'They arrive just before dawn in their boats.'
questions.hint: Look for a time word near the fishermen sentence.

questions.prompt: What does Mei put the fish on?
questions.explanation: 'She arranges the fish on beds of crushed ice.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence about arranging the fish.

questions.prompt: What does Mei write prices on?
questions.explanation: '…writes the prices on a small chalkboard.'
questions.hint: What does Mei use to show prices?

questions.prompt: Who are some of Mei's early customers?
questions.explanation: 'Some are chefs from local restaurants who need fresh fish for the lunchtime menu.'
questions.hint: Look for a word connected with restaurants.

questions.prompt: How does Mei wrap each purchase?
questions.explanation: 'She wraps each purchase in paper and ties it with string.'

questions.prompt: By what time is most of the fish sold?
questions.explanation: 'By nine o'clock, most of the fish is gone.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence with 'most of the fish is gone'.

questions.prompt: How does Mei feel at the end of her morning?
questions.explanation: 'She is tired, but she is happy.'
questions.hint: Look at the last few sentences.

questions.prompt: What makes Mei's work special compared to a normal shop?
options: She sells the most expensive fish
Her fish comes from far away
Her work ends before most people start their day
questions.explanation: 'Her day's work is done before many people have had their breakfast.'
questions.hint: Look at the very last sentence.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mei's family has run this stall for three generations. What does it mean to inherit a skill or trade from your family? Is this kind of continuity disappearing in modern life — and what, if anything, is lost when it goes?
